sound_type: buck rut grunt -- low guttural 'uurrh', territorial vocalization. 50-200 Hz. Repeated during fall rut. The sound of controlled assertion. E=4
ecosystem: Forest, forest edge, meadow. Fall rut November-December. Territorial patrol.
season: Fall rut November-December. Antlers hardened. Territorial behavior peak.

coordinates.description: Black-tailed Deer throughout western Washington. Forest and forest edge. Ubiquitous.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Black-tailed Deer entry. First at 195 (fawn bleat, E=3). Fawn-buck comparison pair across degrees 195-196. Same species, opposite life stages.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Cervidae: Black-tailed Deer Fawn Bleat (195, E=3), Black-tailed Deer Buck Grunt (196, E=4).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Odocoileus from Greek 'odous' (tooth) + 'koilos' (hollow). hemionus from Greek 'hemi' (half) + 'onos' (donkey). columbianus for Columbia River region.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Odocoileus -- White-tailed and Mule Deer (including Black-tailed subspecies).
taxonomy_note.family_position: Cervidae: second entry. Fawn-buck pair at 195-196.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Terrestrial. First Cervidae pair complete.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Buck grunt: 50-200 Hz. Snort alarm: 200-800 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Grunt: 0.3-1.0 sec. Repeated at 5-30 second intervals.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single guttural grunt per vocalization. Repeated pattern during territorial patrol.
song_metrics.note_types: Buck grunt (territorial, low), snort (alarm, higher, both sexes).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Repeated grunts at regular intervals. Intensity increases as rut peaks or during rival encounters.
song_metrics.timbre: Low, guttural, resonant. Vibrates in the chest cavity. Forceful but controlled.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Territorial. Controlled assertion. More forceful than fawn bleat (E=3). Carries 100-300 m in forest.
song_metrics.tempo: Grunts at 5-30 second intervals. Tempo increases during encounters.
song_metrics.context: Fall rut (November-December). Territorial patrol. Buck rubs. Scrapes. Doe attraction. Pre-sparring.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Buck grunts structurally similar across Odocoileus populations.
song_metrics.key_property: BUCK RUT GRUNT. Low guttural. 50-200 Hz. Territorial. FAWN-BUCK PAIR (195-196). E=4. ENERGY MATCH with 764-HERO. v1.49.337.

pelage.overall: Adult buck: grayish-brown (winter) to reddish-brown (summer). Black-topped white tail. Antlers (mature buck: 3-4 points per side). Muscular neck thickens during rut.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Antlers (bucks, fall). Black-topped tail. Muscular build. Thickened neck during rut. Buck rubs on trees.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Summer: reddish-brown. Winter: grayish-brown. Antlers: spring growth through fall hardening to winter shedding. Neck thickens during rut.

physical_description.overall: Adult buck: 60-90 kg (130-200 lbs). 80-100 cm at shoulder. Antlers: up to 4 points per side (mature). Muscular. Black-topped tail.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Antlers. Black-topped tail. Reddish-brown (summer) or gray-brown (winter). Buck rubs on trees during rut.
physical_description.field_id: Medium deer with antlers (bucks, fall-winter). Black on top of white tail. Look for buck rubs on small trees (bark stripped 0.5-1.5 m high). During rut: thickened neck, active movement, low grunting.

nodes.title: Fawn-Buck Pair -- 195-196
nodes.description: Same species across consecutive degrees. Fawn bleat (E=3, high, need) and buck grunt (E=4, low, claim). The full vocal range of one species in two degrees.

nodes.title: Territorial Vocalization -- Controlled Assertion
nodes.description: Low guttural grunt at 50-200 Hz. 100-300 m carry in forest. Repeated during rut. Dominance through control, not volume. E=4.

nodes.title: Antler Cycle -- Fastest Tissue Growth
nodes.description: 1-2 cm/day growth. Spring velvet to fall hardening to winter shedding. Antlers, grunts, and territorial behavior form a coordinated system.

nodes.title: Fall Rut -- November-December
nodes.description: Breeding season. Buck rubs, scrapes, scent marking, grunting, sparring. Annual peak of territorial behavior.

cross_references.relationship: FAWN-BUCK PAIR! Same species, consecutive degrees. Fawn E=3 (need) to buck E=4 (claim). High to low. Infant to adult.
TERRITORIAL COMPARISON! Sea lion roar (E=6, marine) vs. buck grunt (E=4, terrestrial). Both assert dominance. Different scales.
INFANT-ADULT PATTERN! Pup whimper (E=3) parallels fawn bleat (E=3). Buck grunt (E=4) is the adult counterpoint across families.
ENERGY MATCH! Both E=4. Restraint (764-HERO) and territory (buck grunt). Controlled expression.
